headline: Microsoft $1B Kenya datacenter project stalls — talks with Ruto government break down over capacity payment guarantees
summary: Microsoft and partner G42's $1B East Africa datacenter project has been delayed after Kenya's government declined to guarantee minimum annual capacity payments at the level Microsoft requested, Bloomberg reported Sunday. Kenya's information PS John Tanui said the project is 'not failed or withdrawn' and is still being structured. The facility was set to run on geothermal power and host an Azure East Africa cloud region. Highlights sovereign-payment-risk friction in hyperscaler offshore expansion.

headline: Anthropic launches Claude Opus 4.7 with ten finance-agent templates and Moody's MCP integration — tops Vals AI Finance Agent benchmark at 64.4%
summary: Anthropic introduced Claude Opus 4.7 and ten pre-built finance agent templates (comp selection, methodology checks, compliance, etc.) at its invite-only NYC briefing. Moody's data is now accessible inside Claude via MCP, and Claude add-ins are GA in Excel, PowerPoint, and Word. The launch follows a $1.5B JV announced one day prior with Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs. JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Citi, AIG, and Visa cited as production users. Implications for enterprise AI workload growth at MSFT/AWS/GOOGL.

headline: ON Semiconductor Q1 beats — AI datacenter revenue jumps 30%+ sequentially, management projects FY26 AI datacenter revenue to double
summary: ON Semiconductor reported Q1 2026 revenue of $1.513B (+5% YoY) vs $1.49B consensus and adjusted EPS of $0.64 vs $0.61 estimate. AI datacenter segment grew 30%+ sequentially. Management guided FY26 AI datacenter revenue to roughly double YoY and projected margin expansion as power-management content per AI server rises. Confirms the broader theme of analog/power semis benefiting from AI server BOM growth.

headline: Samsung Electronics hits $1T market cap — joins TSMC in trillion-dollar club as Asian AI chip rally extends
summary: Samsung Electronics crossed $1T in market capitalization after a ~750% one-year run driven by HBM4 ramp for NVIDIA Rubin and broader AI memory demand. South Korea's KOSPI hit a record above 7,000. Samsung and SK Hynix are now Asia's #2 and #3 most valuable companies behind TSMC. Samsung is targeting +50% HBM capacity expansion in 2026 and completing its custom HBM4E design by May–June 2026. SK Hynix M15X fab pilot ops begin this month producing HBM3E and HBM4.

headline: AMD Q1 datacenter revenue +57% YoY — Goldman Sachs and Bernstein upgrade to Buy, 20+ desks raise targets; Q2 guide of $11.2B implies 46% growth
summary: AMD posted Q1 revenue of $10.25B and adjusted EPS of $1.37, with datacenter revenue +57% YoY. Q2 guidance of ~$11.2B exceeded consensus. Goldman Sachs and Bernstein upgraded AMD to Buy; at least 20 brokerages raised price targets. JPMorgan called the print a 'crystallization of the structural inflection underway' in server CPU and accelerator growth. Management also raised its server-CPU TAM growth target to 35%+ compounded through 2030 (from 18%), citing AI inference workload mix shift.

headline: AI chip leadership 'changing of the guard' theme accelerates — AMD/INTC/MU surge while NVDA scarcity premium compresses
summary: CNBC reports a notable rotation within AI semis: AMD +~25%, Intel +~25%, and Micron +37% over recent sessions, while NVDA lags as the market prices in a wider field of credible AI silicon suppliers. Alphabet's new TPU capacity is being tested by hyperscalers as a credible inference alternative to NVIDIA GPUs. Asian suppliers now account for ~90% of NVIDIA's production costs (vs 65% last year), reinforcing TSMC/Samsung/SK Hynix leverage in the supply chain.

headline: Nebius acquires Eigen AI for $643M to embed AWQ quantization tech — single-GPU token-output efficiency play
summary: Nebius announced on May 1 the $643M acquisition of Eigen AI, a startup spun out of MIT's Han Song Lab. Eigen's Activation-aware Weight Quantization (AWQ) technology allows a single NVIDIA GPU to output substantially more inference tokens. The deal positions Nebius further into inference-economics differentiation versus CoreWeave (training-skewed) and IREN (infrastructure-only). CoreWeave separately disclosed an extended Meta contract reaching $35.2B total commitments through December 2032.

headline: Pentagon awards AI contracts to 8 Big Tech firms — Anthropic notably shut out over defense-use restrictions
summary: The Pentagon signed agreements with eight Big Tech companies (including Microsoft, Google, xAI) for AI services after declining to award Anthropic a contract over its more restrictive defense-use policies. Separately, Microsoft, Google, and xAI agreed to provide US government with pre-launch model testing access. The split underscores divergent approaches to defense work among frontier AI labs and could shape future hyperscaler federal-revenue trajectories.

headline: Week ahead: April CPI Tuesday, Cisco earnings Wed, Applied Materials Thu — Fed-pause expectations and WFE guide in focus
summary: Pre-market setup for the May 11–15 week. Consensus expects April headline CPI at 3.8% YoY and core at 2.7% (Tuesday); a hot print would reinforce a hawkish FOMC tilt. Cisco reports Wed after close — Street looking for $15.56B revenue and $1.04 EPS, with focus on whether AI orders exceed the $5B+ FY26 outlook and how Splunk logo additions are tracking. Applied Materials reports Thu after close ($2.67 EPS est); Morgan Stanley expects FY26 Semi Systems guidance to rise from +20% to +25%+ on AI WFE strength.

headline: Morgan Stanley lifts semiconductor targets across coverage — GFS to $58, IonQ to $47, cites foundry pricing power and silicon photonics tailwind
summary: Morgan Stanley raised price targets on a broad set of semiconductor names during earnings season, citing continued market strength, potential foundry price increases, and growing optimism around silicon photonics. GlobalFoundries (GFS) target raised to $58 from $47; IonQ to $47 from $38. The note characterizes the AI capex cycle as durable and dismisses bubble concerns based on Q1 hyperscaler capex prints. AMAT FY26 Semi Systems guide expected to rise to +25%+ on Thursday.
